items.question_text: Unless the pilot requests otherwise, which fixes should be used in a high-altitude clearance?
items.explanation: Per 4-1-5, use only fixes shown on high altitude en route charts unless the pilot requests otherwise.

choices.text: Only fixes shown on high altitude en route charts.
Only fixes shown on low altitude en route charts.
Any unpublished fix the controller can describe verbally.
Only destination and departure airports, with no intermediate fixes.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed clearance-limit phraseology: "_____ TO DESTINATION AIRPORT"
items.explanation: Per 4-2-1, the prescribed phraseology begins with "CLEARED."

choices.text: CLEARED

items.question_text: If special clearance delivery instructions are needed, what should you do?
items.explanation: Per 4-2-3, issue specific clearance delivery instructions when appropriate.

choices.text: Issue the specific clearance delivery instructions when appropriate.
Wait for the pilot to ask follow-up questions before giving any delivery instructions.
Withhold special delivery instructions unless the aircraft is military.
Send all delivery instructions through the ATIS instead of issuing them directly.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altitude-assignment phraseology: "CLIMB AND _____ FLIGHT LEVEL TWO FOUR ZERO"
items.explanation: Per 4-2-5, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed anticipated-climb phraseology: "CLIMB AND _____ EXPECT AT TIME OR FIX"
items.explanation: Per 4-3-2, the prescribed phraseology uses "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed SID phraseology: "CLIMB VIA SID EXCEPT _____ ALTITUDE"
items.explanation: Per 4-3-3, the prescribed phraseology is "CLIMB VIA SID EXCEPT MAINTAIN ALTITUDE."

choices.text: MAINTAIN

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed departure-release phraseology: "_____ AIRCRAFT IDENTIFICATION RELEASED FOR DEPARTURE"
items.explanation: Per 4-3-4, the prescribed phraseology begins with "ADVISE."

choices.text: ADVISE

items.question_text: Several aircraft choose to absorb delay on the ground before departure. In what order should clearances normally be issued?
items.explanation: Per 4-3-6, issue departure clearances in the order the original clearance requests were made, if practicable.

choices.text: In the order the original clearance requests were made, if practicable.
In order of aircraft performance so the fastest departures leave first.
In reverse request order so later requests can catch up.
In any order, as long as wake turbulence separation is maintained.

items.question_text: What should you do when departure delays are anticipated?
items.explanation: Per 4-3-7, affected approach control facilities and or towers must be informed of anticipated departure delays.

choices.text: Inform the affected approach control facilities and or towers of the anticipated delays.
Wait until each delayed aircraft calls for taxi before coordinating.
Coordinate only if a pilot specifically asks about the reason for the delay.
Issue the delays by NOTAM rather than by controller-to-controller coordination.

items.question_text: When agreements establish mandatory radar handoff procedures, when must coordination be completed?
items.explanation: Per 4-3-8, coordination needs only be effected in a timely manner prior to transfer of control.

choices.text: In a timely manner prior to transfer of control.
Only after the aircraft checks on with the receiving controller.
At any time before the aircraft lands, because the handoff agreement covers the transfer.
Only if the receiving controller asks for additional coordination.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed VFR departure phraseology: "VFR DEPARTURE AUTHORIZED _____ FACILITY ON FREQUENCY AT FOR CLEARANCE"
items.explanation: Per 4-3-9, the prescribed phraseology uses "CONTACT."

choices.text: CONTACT

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed area-clearance phraseology: "_____ TO FLY SPECIFIED QUADRANT OF WITHIN NUMBER OF MILES MILE RADIUS"
items.explanation: Per 4-4-1, the prescribed phraseology begins with "CLEARED."

choices.text: CLEARED

items.question_text: When clearing a non-RNAV aircraft on a depicted alternative route because part of the airway is unusable, what word must immediately precede that route?
items.explanation: Per 4-4-4, the word "substitute" must immediately precede the depicted alternative route.

choices.text: "Substitute."
"Amended."
"Reroute."
"Bypass."

items.question_text: What vertical separation minimum applies above FL410 in standard domestic operations unless a stated exception applies?
items.explanation: Per 4-5-1, the basic vertical separation standard above FL410 is 2,000 feet unless a listed exception applies.

choices.text: 2,000 feet.
1,000 feet.
3,000 feet.
4,000 feet.

items.question_text: At and below FL410, what altitude pattern applies to an aircraft on a magnetic course from 0 through 179 degrees?
items.explanation: Per 4-5-2, aircraft on magnetic courses from 0 through 179 degrees are assigned odd cardinal altitudes or flight levels.

choices.text: Odd cardinal altitudes or flight levels.
Even cardinal altitudes or flight levels.
Only altitudes ending in 500 feet.
Any altitude chosen by the pilot if radar identified.

items.question_text: If the minimum IFR altitude is at or above 18,000 feet MSL and the altimeter setting is below 29.92, how is the adjusted minimum flight level determined?
items.explanation: Per 4-5-5, add the table adjustment factor to the flight-level equivalent of the minimum altitude.

choices.text: Add the table adjustment factor to the flight-level equivalent of the minimum altitude.
Subtract 500 feet from the minimum altitude to compensate for low pressure.
Use FL180 regardless of the pressure setting once the route is in controlled airspace.
Convert the route to a MOCA-based clearance instead of using flight levels.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed SID crossing phraseology: "CLEARED/PROCEED DIRECT WAYPOINT/FIX _____ WAYPOINT/FIX ALTITUDE THEN CLIMB VIA SID"
items.explanation: Per 4-5-7, the prescribed phraseology uses "CROSS."

choices.text: CROSS

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed anticipated altitude-change phraseology: "AT FIX _____ ALTITUDE/FLIGHT LEVEL CHANGE FROM NAME OF FACILITY"
items.explanation: Per 4-5-8, the prescribed phraseology uses "REQUEST."

choices.text: REQUEST

items.question_text: If practicable, what should you tell an aircraft that will not be able to climb or descend immediately because another facility controls the blocking altitude?
items.explanation: Per 4-5-8, if practicable, tell the pilot when to expect the altitude change or to request it from the controlling facility.

choices.text: When to expect the climb or descent clearance, or to request it from the controlling facility.
Nothing until the altitude becomes available, because advance notice is discouraged.
That the altitude change has been denied for the rest of the flight.
To cancel IFR if an immediate altitude change is needed.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed holding-clearance phraseology: "CLEARED TO FIX _____ DIRECTION AS PUBLISHED"
items.explanation: Per 4-6-1, the prescribed holding clearance uses "HOLD."

choices.text: HOLD

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed holding-reclearance phraseology: "VIA LAST ROUTING _____"
items.explanation: Per 4-6-2, the prescribed phraseology ends with "CLEARED."

choices.text: CLEARED

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed holding-speed phraseology: "MAXIMUM _____ AIRSPEED IS TWO ONE ZERO KNOTS"
items.explanation: Per 4-6-4, the prescribed phraseology uses "HOLDING."

choices.text: HOLDING

items.question_text: When may a visual holding point be used?
items.explanation: Per 4-6-5, it may be used only when the pilot can determine the point by visual reference and is familiar enough with it to use it reliably.

choices.text: Only when the pilot can determine the point by visual reference and is familiar enough with it to use it reliably.
Whenever the controller can see the aircraft and the point from the tower cab.
Only at airports with radar service.
Any time the holding point is within five miles of the airport.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed arrival-clearance phraseology: "_____ TO DESTINATION AIRPORT"
items.explanation: Per 4-7-1, the prescribed phraseology begins with "CLEARED."

choices.text: CLEARED

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed approach-expectation phraseology: "EXPECT V-O-R APPROACH TO _____ THREE TWO"
items.explanation: Per 4-7-5, the prescribed phraseology uses "RUNWAY."

choices.text: RUNWAY

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed phraseology: "AIRPORT AWOS/ASOS WEATHER _____ ON FREQUENCY"
items.explanation: Per 4-7-10, the prescribed phraseology uses "AVAILABLE."

choices.text: AVAILABLE

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed straight-in approach phraseology: "_____ STRAIGHT-IN TYPE APPROACH"
items.explanation: Per 4-8-1, the prescribed phraseology begins with "CLEARED."

choices.text: CLEARED

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed side-step approach phraseology: "_____ I-L-S RUNWAY SEVEN LEFT APPROACH SIDE-STEP TO RUNWAY SEVEN RIGHT"
items.explanation: Per 4-8-7, the prescribed phraseology begins with "CLEARED."

choices.text: CLEARED

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed advisory-frequency phraseology: "CHANGE TO ADVISORY FREQUENCY _____"
items.explanation: Per 4-8-8, the prescribed phraseology uses "APPROVED."

choices.text: APPROVED

items.question_text: A pilot says they are unfamiliar with the approach. Which item is part of the extra information you must provide?
items.explanation: Per 4-8-10, extra approach information for an unfamiliar pilot includes items such as the initial approach altitude.

choices.text: The initial approach altitude.
The exact distance from every weather reporting point on the airport.
The terrain elevation on each side of the final approach course.
The departure frequency to use after landing.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altitude-advisory phraseology: "MINIMUM SAFE ALTITUDE OR _____ IFR ALTITUDE CRITERIA"
items.explanation: Per 4-8-11, the prescribed phraseology uses "MINIMUM."

choices.text: MINIMUM

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed VFR contact phraseology: "_____ VFR CONTACT TOWER"
items.explanation: Per 4-8-12, the prescribed phraseology begins with "MAINTAIN."

choices.text: MAINTAIN
